description_long: This is a World War II-era single-propeller military airplane with a camouflage paint scheme of green, brown, and yellow, featuring roundels and insignia on the wings and tail. The aircraft has a streamlined fuselage, a single engine mounted at the nose with a visible propeller, and a cockpit situated just behind the engine, likely for a single pilot. The wings are straight and low-mounted, with rounded tips and no visible winglets, and the tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The landing gear is fixed and consists of two main wheels under the wings and a smaller tail wheel. Markings such as "F-G-50" are visible on the fuselage, indicating a specific model or registration. The overall design suggests a robust, maneuverable aircraft suitable for reconnaissance or light combat roles.
description: A WWII-era single-propeller military airplane with camouflage paint, low-mounted straight wings, and fixed landing gear. Features include a single cockpit, visible propeller, and distinctive roundel markings.
description_view_0: Rear view showing the full wingspan, tail section, and camouflage pattern on the wings and fuselage.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the propeller, cockpit canopy, fixed landing gear, and 'F-G-50' marking on the fuselage.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the nose-mounted propeller, cockpit placement, and symmetrical wing structure.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the propeller, cockpit, and tail details with camouflage pattern.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with straight, rounded wings and no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a single tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a clear canopy just behind the engine.
